rulings.Case Title: MILA POPOV VS. BRYAN KELTNER ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 30, 2024 line 1. DEFENDANT SANKAET PATHAK, SYNAPSE FINANCIAL TECH, INC. DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is continued to May 24, 2024. No later than May 15, 2024 Plaintiff to file a supplemental brief limited to citation to causes (no argument) involving similar facts re: subsequent real property owner's complaint as to causes of action accrued during the prior ownership. Plaintiff shall list up to 5 cases on point. If Plaintiff cannot locate any cases on point, Plaintiff shall state in the supplemental brief "No cases located." No further argument to be included. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: U.S. BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION, AS TRUSTEE FOR THE VS. 6-8 NOTTINGHAM, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For An Order Approving Purchase And Sale Agreement And Authorizing The Receiver To Complete The Sale Of The Real Property; Declaration Of Garrick Vanderfin
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 30, 2024 line 2. PLAINTIFF U.S. BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION, AS TRUSTEE FOR THE MOTION FOR AN ORDER APPROVING PURCHASE AND SALE AGREEMENT AND AUTHORIZING THE RECEIVER TO COMPLETE THE SALE OF THE REAL PROPERTY; DECLARATION OF GARRICK VANDERFIN is OFF CALENDAR. Insufficient notice per CCP 1005, 1013 and 1010.6. No courtesy copies received. SFRL 2.7B. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SOMALIA FOLASHADE ET AL VS. THE SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 30, 2024 line 3. DEFENDANT FILLMORE CENTER ASSOCIATES, LLC DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend based on uncertainty. Plaintiffs shall have 60 days from notice of entry of order to file an amended complaint written in clear and concise language specifying who the Plaintiffs are; who each of the defendants are; the relationship between each defendant and each plaintiff; clearly labeling which causes of action are asserted by each Plaintiff and against each defendant; clearly indicating which factual allegations support which cause of action. The Court notes that the First Amended Complaint references exhibits that are not attached, rendering the pleading uncertain. The Court further notes that purported factual allegations in support of the causes of action asserted in the First Amended Complaint appear AFTER the signature and prayer. The Court finally notes that the Prayer only references one "plaintiff" in the singular and refers to the "plaintiff" as "her." =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: VOZIKES 2017 LLC VS. STEVEN ARATA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 30, 2024 line 6. DEFENDANT STEVEN ARATA DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. Operative Notice is fatally defective as it fails to notify the tenant of the deadline to comply with the payment requirement, which is "three days' notice, excluding Saturdays and Sundays and other judicial holidays." =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
